next_steps: Send CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis PCR
Send serum and CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis and Gnathostoma spp. antibody testing
MRI of the brain and spine with contrast to evaluate for track signs or nerve root enhancement
Full infectious disease workup including serum and CSF cryptococcal antigen, VDRL, and AFB culture
Consider anthelmintic therapy and continuation of corticosteroids depending on diagnostic results
Monitor intracranial pressure and neurological status closely
Send CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis NAAT/PCR
Send serum and CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis and Gnathostoma spp. antibody testing
MRI of brain and spine with gadolinium to assess for nerve root enhancement or track signs
Continue corticosteroids and maintain close monitoring of intracranial pressure
Perform detailed dietary history interview focusing on ingestion of raw or undercooked mollusks, slugs, or contaminated produce in Hawaii and Thailand
Repeat lumbar puncture if clinical deterioration occurs for cell counts and to relieve intracranial pressure
Perform comprehensive infectious disease serologies and cultures (cryptococcal antigen, AFB, VDRL, and fungal studies)
